title: Ellisium
summary: Ellisium is a privacy-preserving dark pool on Solana that encrypts order data from submission through settlement and matches orders privately inside Arcium's distributed MPC network.
description:
Ellisium is an Arcium-native trading surface: the homepage and docs both describe it as a privacy-preserving dark pool built on Solana using Arcium's MPC technology. Orders are encrypted client-side, matched privately in Arcium's distributed network, and only settlement results are published on-chain.

The current public deployment is still testnet-only and centers on a SOL/USDC market, but the implementation is already explicit enough to treat the Arcium relationship as confirmed rather than inferred.

bodySections.title: Execution_Flow
bodySections.body: The public product flow is straightforward: connect a Solana wallet, submit encrypted orders, match them inside Arcium's distributed MPC network, and settle the final result on Solana. Ellisium highlights MEV protection, self-custody, client-side encryption, and end-to-end privacy from submission to settlement.

bodySections.title: Coverage_Note
bodySections.body: Atlas should present Ellisium as a confirmed Arcium integration, but still call out that the public interface remains testnet only. The trade page currently shows a SOL/USDC market and explicitly warns against using real funds.

statusNote: Ellisium's own website and docs explicitly say the product is built on Solana using Arcium MPC, but the public trading interface is still testnet only.
